## How This File Works

This file lets you configure project customization offline.
Fill in the values below, check the boxes for options you want,
then apply your choices:

1. Edit the **Value** column in the Project Identity table below.
2. Check the boxes (`[x]`) for options you want to enable.
3. Preview: `python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md --dry-run`
4. Apply: `python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md`

> **Tip:** In VS Code, checkboxes are clickable in the Markdown preview.
> Clicking a checkbox toggles it in the underlying file. Ensure the setting
> `markdown.editor.toggleCheckboxInPreview` is enabled (it is on by default).
> Alternatively, edit the raw Markdown: change `[ ]` to `[x]` (or vice versa).

> **Tip:** For a fully non-interactive approach without editing this file,
> use `python scripts/customize.py --non-interactive --project-name NAME
> --author NAME --github-user NAME` with all values on the command line.

---

## Project Identity

Fill in **your** project values. Leave a field empty to keep the template default.

| Setting | Value | Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Project name** | `simple-python-boilerplate` | Lowercase, hyphens OK (e.g. `my-cool-app`). Used as repo slug and PyPI name. |
| **Package name** | `simple_python_boilerplate` | Python import name, underscored. Auto-derived from project name if left as default. |
| **Author** | `Joseph` | Author / maintainer name for pyproject.toml. |
| **GitHub user** | `JoJo275` | GitHub username or organization. |
| **Description** | `Simple Python boilerplate using src/ layout` | One-line project description. |
| **CLI prefix** | `spb` | CLI command prefix for entry points (e.g. `spb-version`). Default: initials of project name. |

---

## License

A license defines how others may use, modify, and distribute your code.
Without one, your project is "all rights reserved" by default — no one
can legally reuse it. Open-source licenses (MIT, Apache, BSD, etc.) grant
explicit permissions and are expected by package registries like PyPI.

Pick **one** license by changing the checkbox to `[x]`:

- [x] **Apache License 2.0** (`apache-2.0`)
- [ ] **MIT License** (`mit`)
- [ ] **BSD 3-Clause License** (`bsd-3-clause`)

---

## Repository Visibility

- [ ] **Private repository** — Strip open-source community files not needed
  for private repos (CODE_OF_CONDUCT, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY, scorecard
  workflow, etc.).

> **Note:** Private repositories on GitHub have a limited amount of Actions
> minutes per month (e.g. 2,000 min/month on the Free plan). CI/CD workflows
> can consume these quickly — especially matrix builds, container builds, and
> nightly scheduled jobs. Consider disabling non-essential workflows or moving
> heavy jobs to self-hosted runners to stay within your quota.

Files that would be removed:

- **Open-source community files (not needed for private repos)**
  - `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`
  - `CONTRIBUTING.md`
  - `SECURITY.md`
  - `pgp-key.asc`
- **Public-repo workflows (Scorecard, welcome bot, stale issues)**
  - `.github/workflows/scorecard.yml`
  - `.github/workflows/welcome.yml`
  - `.github/workflows/stale.yml`
  - `.github/workflows/sbom.yml`
- **GitHub label management (labels/, apply scripts)**
  - `labels/`
  - `scripts/apply_labels.py`
  - `scripts/apply-labels.sh`

## Items to Remove

Select directories and files you do **not** need.
You can recover anything from git history if needed.

> Directories are deleted **dynamically** — any new files added to a
> directory after this config was generated will also be removed when
> applied. See the [Repository Layout](#repository-layout) to preview
> the full file tree.

### Bulk Actions

> Use these to apply actions to all items at once without
> checking individual boxes below.

- [ ] **Select all items below** (`select-all`) — Mark all items for deletion (directory + files)
- [ ] **Delete only files in all items** (`select-all-files-only`) — Delete files in all items but keep empty directory structures

> Or bypass individual selection entirely:

- [ ] **Delete ALL directories regardless of selection** (`delete-all-dirs`) — Delete every optional directory and file listed below
- [ ] **Delete ALL files only regardless of selection** (`delete-all-files`) — Delete files in every optional directory but keep directory structures

### Optional Directories & Files

For each item, check the main box to **delete the directory and all files**.
Optionally, also check the "files only" sub-option to **keep the directory**
structure but remove all files inside.

<a id="item-db"></a>

- [ ] **Database directory (schema, migrations, seeds, queries)** (`db`) — [details ↓](#detail-db)
  - [ ] Files only — keep empty directory structure (`db:files-only`)
  - `db/` *(8 files — all contents deleted dynamically)*
  - `scripts/sql/` *(3 files — all contents deleted dynamically)*
  - *+ 1 more directories*

<a id="item-experiments"></a>

- [ ] **Experiments / scratch code directory** (`experiments`) — [details ↓](#detail-experiments)

## Item Details & Trade-offs

Detailed descriptions and trade-offs for each removable item.
Read these before making your selections in [Items to Remove](#items-to-remove).

---

### Optional Directories

<a id="detail-db"></a>

#### `db` — Database directory (schema, migrations, seeds, queries)

> **What it is:** Database schema definitions, migration scripts, SQL seed data, and example queries for SQL-based projects.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose database scaffolding (schema, migrations, seeds). Must recreate from scratch if you add a database layer later.

**Files affected:**
- `db/` ✅
- `scripts/sql/` ✅
- `src/simple_python_boilerplate/sql/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-db)

---

<a id="detail-experiments"></a>

#### `experiments` — Experiments / scratch code directory

> **What it is:** Notebook-style scratch code area for testing ideas, prototyping APIs, and exploring data without polluting src/.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose a dedicated scratch-work area. You'll need to create a new directory for ad-hoc experiments.

**Files affected:**
- `experiments/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-experiments)

---

<a id="detail-var"></a>

#### `var` — Local data / state directory (var/)

> **What it is:** Local runtime data such as SQLite databases, log files, and application state. Gitignored for sensitive data.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose the sample SQLite database and the convention for runtime data storage. Scripts that reference var/ will break.

**Files affected:**
- `var/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-var)

---

<a id="detail-docs-templates"></a>

#### `docs-templates` — Security-policy & issue templates (docs/templates/)

> **What it is:** Pre-built security policy template and GitHub issue/PR templates for standardized contributor experience.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose pre-written templates. You'll need to write issue and PR templates from scratch if you want them later.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/templates/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-docs-templates)

---

<a id="detail-container"></a>

#### `container` — Container files (Containerfile, docker-compose, build & scan workflows)

> **What it is:** Multi-stage Containerfile, docker-compose for local development, container structure tests, and CI workflows for building and scanning container images.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose container deployment setup including multi-stage builds, security scanning, and structure tests. Significant effort to recreate.

**Files affected:**
- `Containerfile` ✅
- `docker-compose.yml` ✅
- `container-structure-test.yml` ✅
- `.containerignore` ✅
- `.dockerignore` ✅
- `.github/workflows/container-build.yml` ✅
- `.github/workflows/container-scan.yml` ✅
- `scripts/_container_common.py` ✅
- `scripts/test_containerfile.py` ✅
- `scripts/test_containerfile.sh` ✅
- `scripts/test_docker_compose.py` ✅
- `scripts/test_docker_compose.sh` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-container)

---

<a id="detail-optional-workflows"></a>

#### `optional-workflows` — Optional workflow templates (.github/workflows-optional/)

> **What it is:** Extra GitHub Actions workflow templates that can be moved into .github/workflows/ when needed (e.g. nightly builds, performance benchmarks).

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose pre-built optional workflow templates. Can be recreated from GitHub Actions documentation if needed.

**Files affected:**
- `.github/workflows-optional/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-optional-workflows)

---

<a id="detail-labels"></a>

#### `labels` — Label management files (labels/, apply scripts)

> **What it is:** Standardized GitHub label definitions (baseline + extended sets) and scripts to apply them via the GitHub API.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose automated label management. Must configure GitHub labels manually through the web UI.

**Files affected:**
- `labels/` ✅
- `scripts/apply_labels.py` ✅
- `scripts/apply-labels.sh` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-labels)

---

<a id="detail-repo-doctor"></a>

#### `repo-doctor` — Repo doctor checks (repo_doctor.d/, doctor scripts)

> **What it is:** Repository health check system with TOML rule profiles for CI, Python, docs, containers, database, and security. Includes environment and git diagnostics.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose automated repository health monitoring and diagnostics. Manual inspection required to catch missing files, config drift, and structural issues.

**Files affected:**
- `repo_doctor.d/` ✅
- `scripts/repo_doctor.py` ✅
- `scripts/doctor.py` ✅
- `scripts/env_doctor.py` ✅
- `scripts/git_doctor.py` ✅
- `scripts/_doctor_common.py` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-repo-doctor)

---

<a id="detail-mkdocs-hooks"></a>

#### `mkdocs-hooks` — MkDocs hooks (mkdocs-hooks/)

> **What it is:** Build-time MkDocs hooks that auto-generate command references, resolve repo links, and include template content during documentation builds.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** MkDocs builds will fail if hooks are referenced in mkdocs.yml. Remove hook entries from mkdocs.yml before or after stripping.

**Files affected:**
- `mkdocs-hooks/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-mkdocs-hooks)

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose repo analytics dashboard. No built-in replacement for at-a-glance repository statistics.

- `scripts/repo_sauron.py` ✅
- `repo-sauron-report.md` ✅

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose reference material. Consult official Git docs or create your own reference instead.

- `git-config-reference.md` ✅

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose test setup reference. Consult pytest docs or pyproject.toml [tool.pytest] for configuration.

- `test-config-ref.md` ✅

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose automated release config. Must set up release automation from scratch if needed later.

- `release-please-config.json` ✅

> **What it is:** Traditional pip requirements files. Redundant when using Hatch with pyproject.toml for dependency management.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose pip-compatible requirements files. Some deployment tools still expect requirements.txt — recreate with 'pip freeze' or 'hatch dep show requirements' if needed.

> **What it is:** Configuration files for markdownlint-cli2, Prettier ignore patterns, and EditorConfig cross-editor formatting. Only needed if using these linting/formatting tools.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose consistent cross-editor formatting (.editorconfig) and markdown lint rules. Some pre-commit hooks may fail without these configs — update .pre-commit-config.yaml accordingly.

**Files affected:**
- `.markdownlint-cli2.jsonc` ✅
- `.prettierignore` ✅
- `.editorconfig` ✅

<a id="detail-link-checker"></a>

#### `link-checker` — Link checker ignore list (.lycheeignore)

> **What it is:** Ignore list for the Lychee link checker, used by CI to skip known-broken or intentionally-unreachable URLs.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lychee link-check CI jobs may report false positives for URLs that were previously ignored. Recreate if needed.

**Files affected:**
- `.lycheeignore` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-link-checker)

---

<a id="detail-git-templates"></a>

#### `git-templates` — Git config templates (.gitconfig.recommended, .gitmessage.txt)

> **What it is:** Recommended Git configuration and commit message template. Useful for onboarding but not required for the project to work.

> ⚠️ **Downsides:** Lose recommended git aliases, merge strategies, and commit message format. Developers must configure git manually.

**Files affected:**
- `.gitconfig.recommended` ✅
- `.gitmessage.txt` ✅

### Template Cleanup Items

<a id="detail-adr-files"></a>

#### `adr-files` — ADR files (docs/adr/ — template design decisions)

> ⚠ Removes all numbered ADR files and subdirectories (e.g. archive/) from docs/adr/. Keeps README.md and template.md. These ADRs document decisions made for the TEMPLATE repository — why Ruff over Black, why src/ layout, why Hatch, etc. They do NOT describe decisions in YOUR project. Removing them deletes the rationale behind the tooling and patterns you inherited, which can make it harder for future contributors to understand why things are set up this way. Consider keeping them as reference until you've written your own ADRs.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/adr/001-src-layout.md` ✅
- `docs/adr/002-pyproject-toml.md` ✅
- `docs/adr/003-separate-workflow-files.md` ✅

- `docs/adr/007-mypy-for-type-checking.md` ✅

- `docs/adr/009-conventional-commits.md` ✅

---

<a id="detail-docs-notes"></a>

#### `docs-notes` — Developer notes (docs/notes/ — learning, resources, TODOs, etc.)

> ⚠ Removes the entire docs/notes/ directory, including: learning.md (Python packaging lessons, mental models for Hatch vs Hatchling, lockfile strategies), resources.md (curated links to official docs for every tool in the stack), tool-comparison.md (side-by-side evaluations of formatters, linters, type checkers, task runners, etc.), todo.md (roadmap and checklist of remaining template work), archive.md (completed items for historical context), and README.md. No project functionality depends on these files, but they contain valuable context — especially learning.md and resources.md — that can accelerate onboarding for developers unfamiliar with the tooling stack. Consider keeping them as reference material, or extracting useful content into your own docs before removing.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/notes/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-docs-notes)

---

<a id="detail-docs-design"></a>

#### `docs-design` — Design docs (docs/design/ — architecture, tool decisions, CI/CD design, database, etc.)

> ⚠ Removes the entire docs/design/ directory, including: architecture.md, ci-cd-design.md, database.md, tool-decisions.md, and any future files added there. These document the 'why' behind CI/CD pipeline design, dependency choices, database schema rationale, and overall system architecture. The copilot-instructions.md file references these docs — Copilot will lose context for architectural questions. Contributors will have no written rationale for the project's structural decisions.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/design/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-docs-design)

---

<a id="detail-docs-reference"></a>

#### `docs-reference` — Reference docs (docs/reference/ — API, commands, inventory, etc.)

> ⚠ Removes the entire docs/reference/ directory, including: api.md, commands.md, index.md, template-inventory.md, and README.md. The command reference is auto-generated by generate_command_reference.py and will need to be regenerated if you add new scripts. The template inventory is only useful during initial setup. The mkdocs-hooks/generate_commands.py hook will fail if docs/reference/ is missing — update mkdocs.yml nav and remove the hook if you strip this.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/reference/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-docs-reference)

---

<a id="detail-docs-development"></a>

#### `docs-development` — Development guides (docs/development/ — dev setup, PR guide, command workflows, etc.)

> ⚠ Removes the entire docs/development/ directory, including: dev-setup.md, pull-requests.md, developer-commands.md, command-workflows.md, development.md, and README.md. These are developer onboarding docs covering environment setup, PR workflow, and available commands. Removing these makes it harder for new contributors to get started. mkdocs.yml nav links to these pages — update it to avoid broken links. Consider replacing with your own project-specific guides.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/development/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-docs-development)

---

<a id="detail-docs-guide"></a>

#### `docs-guide` — User guides (docs/guide/ — getting started, troubleshooting, etc.)

> ⚠ Removes the entire docs/guide/ directory, including: getting-started.md, troubleshooting.md, and README.md. These provide end-user onboarding and common issue resolution. mkdocs.yml nav references these pages — update it to avoid broken links. Consider replacing with project-specific guides rather than deleting outright.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/guide/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-docs-guide)

---

<a id="detail-placeholder-code"></a>

#### `placeholder-code` — Placeholder source & test files (clear src/ and tests/ content)

> ⚠ Replaces placeholder code in src/ modules (api.py, cli.py, engine.py, main.py) and test files (test_example.py) with minimal stubs. Also removes non-__init__.py files from the dev_tools/ subdirectory if present. Keeps the file structure so you can fill in your own code. __init__.py, _version.py, conftest.py, and py.typed are preserved as-is. Entry points in pyproject.toml still reference these files — update them if you rename or restructure.

↑ [Back to selection](#item-placeholder-code)

---

<a id="detail-utility-scripts"></a>

#### `utility-scripts` — Template utility scripts (scripts/ — most helper scripts)

> ⚠ Removes 7 scripts and the precommit/ directory: archive_todos.py, changelog_check.py, check_known_issues.py, check_todos.py, dep_versions.py, generate_command_reference.py, workflow_versions.py, and scripts/precommit/. bootstrap.py, clean.py, and customize.py are kept. Removing these also means Taskfile tasks and pre-commit hooks that call them will break — update Taskfile.yml and .pre-commit-config.yaml accordingly. Some GitHub Actions workflows may also reference these scripts.

**Files affected:**
- `scripts/archive_todos.py` ✅
- `scripts/changelog_check.py` ✅
- `scripts/check_known_issues.py` ✅
- `scripts/check_todos.py` ✅
- `scripts/dep_versions.py` ✅
- `scripts/generate_command_reference.py` ✅
- `scripts/workflow_versions.py` ✅
- `scripts/precommit/` ✅

↑ [Back to selection](#item-utility-scripts)

---

<a id="detail-advanced-workflows"></a>

#### `advanced-workflows` — Advanced workflows (keep only essential CI subset)

> ⚠ Deletes ALL .yml files in .github/workflows/ EXCEPT the essential set: ci-gate, test, lint-format, type-check, coverage, security-audit, dependency-review, docs-build. This removes ~28 workflows including: release automation (release-please), container builds & scans, nightly security scans, Scorecard, SBOM generation, spellcheck, label management, welcome bot, stale issues, and more. You will lose significant CI/CD automation — including automated releases and container publishing. Non-.yml files (e.g. .instructions.md) are kept. You can re-add individual workflows from git history or the .github/workflows-optional/ directory.

↑ [Back to selection](#item-advanced-workflows)

---

## Script Recommended

While this Markdown file provides a visual way to configure customization,
the **recommended approach for automation and reproducibility** is to use
the script directly:

```bash
# Interactive wizard (guided step-by-step)
python scripts/customize.py

# Non-interactive (CI/automation friendly)
python scripts/customize.py --non-interactive \
    --project-name my-project \
    --author "Your Name" \
    --github-user yourusername \
    --strip db experiments var \
    --template-cleanup adr-files docs-notes placeholder-code
```

**Advantages of the script over this file:**

| Feature | Script | This file |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Validation | ✅ Real-time input validation | ❌ No validation until apply |
| Dynamic discovery | ✅ Always current file lists | ⚠️ Snapshot at generation time |
| Automation | ✅ `--non-interactive` flag | ❌ Requires manual editing |
| Dry run | ✅ `--dry-run` flag | ✅ `--dry-run` flag |
| Reproducibility | ✅ Full CLI args | ⚠️ File-based config |

---

## Quick Reference

| Command | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- |
| `python scripts/customize.py` | Generate this config file |
| `python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md --dry-run` | Preview changes |
| `python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md` | Apply changes |
| `python scripts/customize.py --non-interactive --project-name NAME ...` | Direct CLI mode |
| `python scripts/customize.py --enable-workflows owner/repo` | Enable workflows only |
| `python scripts/customize.py --export-config custom-path.md` | Export to custom path |

---

## Applying Changes from This File

Edit the values and checkboxes above, then apply:

```bash
# Preview changes first
python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md --dry-run

# Apply changes
python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md
```

**How it works:**

- Values in the Project Identity table replace the template placeholders.
- Checked boxes (`[x]`) activate that option (strip, cleanup, private repo).
- Unchecked boxes (`[ ]`) leave that option inactive.
- **Bulk actions** override individual selections when checked.
- **Files only** options delete files but preserve directory structures.
- **Directories delete dynamically** — all contents are removed, including files added after this config was generated.
- Use `--dry-run` to preview all changes before applying.
- Use `--force` to skip the already-customized safety check.
